county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Columbia County formed 1846 from Portage County. County seat is Portage, located at the historic Fox-Wisconsin portage route critical to the fur trade. Mixed Yankee, German, Norwegian, and Scottish settlement.
county_metadata.research_tips: Columbia County Register of Deeds in Portage holds land records from 1846. The city of Portage sits at the historic Fox-Wisconsin portage route -- a critical fur trade corridor. Scottish, English, German, and Norwegian immigrants settled here. The Columbia County Historical Society in Portage has extensive collections. WHS has microfilmed Columbia County records. Zona Gale and Frederick Jackson Turner were from Portage -- their papers at WHS contain local references. The Surgeon's Quarters museum in Portage has frontier-era records. FamilySearch has indexed some Columbia County records. BLM GLO patents for original federal land entries. Strong church records (Methodist, Presbyterian, Lutheran, Catholic).
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO federal land patents for Columbia County; county deed books at the Register of Deeds office in Portage; WHS has microfilm of historical deed books

source_profiles.source_name: Columbia County Deed Books -- Register of Deeds
source_profiles.repository: Columbia County Register of Deeds, Portage
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Original deed books for Columbia County from 1846; grantor/grantee indexes; mortgages and liens; Register of Deeds office maintains land records. WHS has microfilm of historical deed books
source_profiles.survival_notes: Excellent survival from 1846; no major record losses

source_profiles.repository: Bureau of Land Management, General Land Office Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal land patents (preemption, homestead, cash entry, military bounty) for Columbia County; Wisconsin is a public land state and GLO patents document original federal land transfers to private ownership

source_profiles.repository: Columbia County Probate Court, Portage
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Wills, administrations, inventories, guardianships, and estate settlements for Columbia County from 1846; Probate Court has jurisdiction over estate matters; WHS has microfilm of historical probate records

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Crawford County formed 1818 as one of Wisconsin's two original counties (with Brown, under Michigan Territory). County seat is Prairie du Chien, one of the oldest European settlements in the upper Midwest. French fur trade heritage from the 1600s. Fort Crawford military post.
county_metadata.research_tips: Crawford County Register of Deeds in Prairie du Chien holds land records from 1818 -- one of Wisconsin's two original counties (with Brown). Prairie du Chien is one of the oldest European settlements in the upper Midwest, with French fur trade records from the 1600s. Fort Crawford military records at NARA. The Crawford County Historical Society (Prairie du Chien Museum at Fort Crawford) has excellent local collections. WHS has microfilmed Crawford County records. French-Canadian, British, and mixed-heritage (Metis) families from the fur trade era are documented in church records (St. Gabriel's Catholic Church from 1817) and fur trade company papers at WHS. FamilySearch has indexed some Crawford County records.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO federal land patents for Crawford County; county deed books at the Register of Deeds office in Prairie du Chien; WHS has microfilm of historical deed books

source_profiles.survival_notes: Excellent survival from 1818; no major record losses

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Dane County formed 1836 from Iowa and Milwaukee counties. County seat is Madison, the state capital since territorial organization. University of Wisconsin-Madison. Wisconsin Historical Society headquarters. Strong Norwegian, German, and Yankee settlement.
county_metadata.research_tips: Dane County Register of Deeds in Madison holds land records from 1836. The state capital county with excellent archival resources. WHS is headquartered in Madison and holds the most comprehensive genealogical collections in the state. University of Wisconsin-Madison archives and special collections. Dane County Historical Society. The Wisconsin State Law Library has court records. Very strong Norwegian immigrant settlement -- Little Norway historic site preserves this heritage. German, Irish, and Yankee settlers also prominent. Madison city directories from the 1850s. State government employee records at WHS. FamilySearch has extensive Dane County collections. BLM GLO patents for original federal land entries. Forest Hill Cemetery and other burial grounds have detailed records.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Dane County Register of Deeds online index and images; historical deed books from 1836

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Dodge County formed 1836 from Brown County. County seat is Juneau (city). One of Wisconsin's earliest organized counties. Very strong German (Catholic and Lutheran) settlement. Horicon Marsh.
county_metadata.research_tips: Dodge County Register of Deeds in Juneau holds land records from 1836. One of the earliest organized counties. Very strong German (predominantly Catholic and Lutheran) settlement -- Dodge County had one of the highest proportions of German-born residents in Wisconsin. The Dodge County Historical Society in Beaver Dam has extensive collections including family files. WHS has microfilmed Dodge County records. Horicon Marsh area records. Catholic parishes in the Archdiocese of Milwaukee and Diocese of Madison hold vital parish registers. Lutheran church records (Missouri Synod, Wisconsin Synod) are also critical. FamilySearch has indexed many Dodge County records. BLM GLO patents. The Juneau County Courthouse should not be confused with the city of Juneau which is the Dodge County seat.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO federal land patents for Dodge County; county deed books at the Register of Deeds office in Juneau; WHS has microfilm of historical deed books

source_profiles.survival_notes: Excellent survival from 1836; no major record losses

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Door County formed 1851 from Brown County. County seat is Sturgeon Bay. The Door Peninsula extends into Lake Michigan. Belgian (Walloon), Scandinavian, and German settlement. Maritime and cherry-farming heritage. Affected by the 1871 fires.
county_metadata.research_tips: Door County Register of Deeds in Sturgeon Bay holds land records from 1851. The Door Peninsula extends into Lake Superior -- maritime and cherry-farming heritage. Very strong Belgian (Walloon), Scandinavian (Norwegian, Swedish, Icelandic), and German immigrant communities. The Door County Historical Museum and Maritime Museum in Sturgeon Bay have excellent collections. The Great Peshtigo Fire of 1871 also affected Door County (Williamsonville fire) -- some early records may have been lost. WHS has microfilmed Door County records. Belgian Heritage Center in Brussels, WI documents the Walloon settlement. Church records (Catholic Belgian parishes, Moravian, Lutheran) are critical. FamilySearch has indexed Door County records.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO federal land patents for Door County; county deed books at the Register of Deeds office in Sturgeon Bay; WHS has microfilm of historical deed books

source_profiles.survival_notes: Excellent survival from 1851; no major record losses

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Douglas County formed 1854 from La Pointe (unorganized territory). County seat is Superior, a major Great Lakes port city. Finnish, Swedish, Norwegian, Polish, Italian, and Slavic immigrant communities. Strong labor movement history.
county_metadata.research_tips: Douglas County Register of Deeds in Superior holds land records from 1854. Superior was a major Great Lakes port -- shipping, railroad, and industrial records document immigrant workers (Finnish, Swedish, Norwegian, Polish, Italian, Slavic). The Douglas County Historical Society in Superior has extensive collections. WHS has microfilmed Douglas County records. Superior Public Library has local history collections including city directories. Finnish-American Heritage Center materials (also at University of Minnesota Immigration History Research Center). Strong labor and socialist movement records -- Superior was a center of Finnish socialist organizations. FamilySearch has indexed some Douglas County records. Port records and naturalization records at NARA Great Lakes Region.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO federal land patents for Douglas County; county deed books at the Register of Deeds office in Superior; WHS has microfilm of historical deed books

source_profiles.survival_notes: Excellent survival from 1854; no major record losses

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Dunn County formed 1854 from Chippewa County. County seat is Menomonie. Western Wisconsin lumber industry county. Knapp, Stout & Co. lumber operations. University of Wisconsin-Stout. Norwegian and German settlement.
county_metadata.research_tips: Dunn County Register of Deeds in Menomonie holds land records from 1854. Western Wisconsin county with strong lumber industry heritage -- James Stout and the Knapp, Stout & Co. lumber operations dominated the economy. University of Wisconsin-Stout archives hold lumber company and local records. Norwegian and German settlers were the primary immigrant groups. WHS has microfilmed Dunn County records. The Dunn County Historical Society in Menomonie maintains local collections. Russell J. Rassbach Heritage Museum. Church records (Norwegian Lutheran, German Lutheran, Catholic) are important supplements. FamilySearch has indexed some Dunn County records. BLM GLO patents for original federal land entries.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO federal land patents for Dunn County; county deed books at the Register of Deeds office in Menomonie; WHS has microfilm of historical deed books

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Eau Claire County formed 1856 from Chippewa County. County seat is Eau Claire at the confluence of the Eau Claire and Chippewa Rivers. Major lumber industry center. University of Wisconsin-Eau Claire. German, Norwegian, and French-Canadian settlement.
county_metadata.research_tips: Eau Claire County Register of Deeds in Eau Claire holds land records from 1856. Major lumber industry center at the confluence of the Eau Claire and Chippewa Rivers. The Chippewa Valley Museum in Eau Claire has excellent genealogical and local history collections. University of Wisconsin-Eau Claire special collections hold regional materials. WHS has microfilmed Eau Claire County records. Strong German, Norwegian, and French-Canadian settlement. Eau Claire city directories from the 1870s. Paul Bunyan Logging Camp Museum preserves lumber heritage. Catholic diocese and Lutheran synod records are important. FamilySearch has indexed some Eau Claire County records. BLM GLO patents.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO federal land patents for Eau Claire County; county deed books at the Register of Deeds office in Eau Claire; WHS has microfilm of historical deed books

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Florence County formed 1882 from Marinette and Oconto counties. County seat is Florence. Northeastern Wisconsin iron mining and logging county. Wisconsin's least populous county. Finnish, Swedish, Italian, and Cornish settlement.
county_metadata.research_tips: Florence County Register of Deeds in Florence holds land records from 1882. Northeastern Wisconsin county created from Marinette and Oconto in 1882 -- check parent counties for earlier records. Iron mining (Florence mine) and logging brought Finnish, Swedish, Italian, and Cornish workers. Wisconsin's least populous county. WHS has microfilmed Florence County records. The Florence County Historical Society in Florence maintains local collections. Wild Rivers Interpretive Center has some historical materials. Mining company records at WHS and Michigan Technological University Archives. FamilySearch has limited Florence County records. BLM GLO patents for original federal land entries.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO federal land patents for Florence County; county deed books at the Register of Deeds office in Florence; WHS has microfilm of historical deed books

source_profiles.source_name: Florence County Deed Books -- Register of Deeds
source_profiles.repository: Florence County Register of Deeds, Florence
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Original deed books for Florence County from 1882; grantor/grantee indexes; mortgages and liens; Register of Deeds office maintains land records. WHS has microfilm of historical deed books
source_profiles.survival_notes: Excellent survival from 1882; no major record losses

source_profiles.source_name: Florence County Federal Land Patents -- BLM GLO
source_profiles.repository: Bureau of Land Management, General Land Office Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal land patents (preemption, homestead, cash entry, military bounty) for Florence County; Wisconsin is a public land state and GLO patents document original federal land transfers to private ownership

source_profiles.source_name: Florence County Probate Court Records
source_profiles.repository: Florence County Probate Court, Florence
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Wills, administrations, inventories, guardianships, and estate settlements for Florence County from 1882; Probate Court has jurisdiction over estate matters; WHS has microfilm of historical probate records

source_profiles.source_name: Florence County Marriage Records -- County Clerk / WHS / FamilySearch
source_profiles.repository: Florence County Clerk, Florence / Wisconsin Historical Society, Madison
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage records for Florence County from 1882; County Clerk issued marriage licenses in Wisconsin; WHS holds some county marriage records on microfilm; FamilySearch has indexed Wisconsin marriages; state vital records registration began 1907 (sporadic earlier compliance)

source_profiles.source_name: Florence County Federal and State Census Records
source_profiles.repository: NARA / FamilySearch / Ancestry / WHS
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal census 1890-1950 for Florence County; Wisconsin state census 1855, 1865, 1875, 1885, 1895, 1905 at WHS (unusually detailed -- include place of birth, occupation, citizenship status); territorial census 1836 and 1842 may include Florence County entries

source_profiles.source_name: Florence County Iron Mining Records -- WHS / Michigan Tech Archives
source_profiles.repository: Wisconsin Historical Society, Madison / Michigan Technological University Archives
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Iron mining company employment records, mine inspector reports, accident records, and payroll records for Florence County mines on the Gogebic/Penokee Range; Finnish, Italian, Polish, Swedish, and Cornish mine workers documented; union records (Western Federation of Miners, UMWA)

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Fond du Lac County formed 1836 from Brown County. County seat is Fond du Lac at the south end of Lake Winnebago. One of Wisconsin's original counties. Very strong German (Catholic) settlement. Dairy farming heritage.
county_metadata.research_tips: Fond du Lac County Register of Deeds in Fond du Lac holds land records from 1836. One of Wisconsin's original counties at the south end of Lake Winnebago. Very strong German immigrant settlement (Fond du Lac was a center of German Catholic life). The Fond du Lac County Historical Society has extensive collections. WHS has microfilmed Fond du Lac County records. The Archdiocese of Milwaukee and Diocese of Fond du Lac hold Catholic parish registers. The Galloway House and Village museum complex in Fond du Lac. Marian University (formerly Marian College) has some local records. FamilySearch has indexed many Fond du Lac County records. BLM GLO patents. Strong Lutheran and Catholic church records.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO federal land patents for Fond du Lac County; county deed books at the Register of Deeds office in Fond du Lac; WHS has microfilm of historical deed books

source_profiles.source_name: Fond du Lac County Deed Books -- Register of Deeds
source_profiles.repository: Fond du Lac County Register of Deeds, Fond du Lac
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Original deed books for Fond du Lac County from 1836; grantor/grantee indexes; mortgages and liens; Register of Deeds office maintains land records. WHS has microfilm of historical deed books
source_profiles.survival_notes: Excellent survival from 1836; no major record losses

source_profiles.source_name: Fond du Lac County Federal Land Patents -- BLM GLO
source_profiles.repository: Bureau of Land Management, General Land Office Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal land patents (preemption, homestead, cash entry, military bounty) for Fond du Lac County; Wisconsin is a public land state and GLO patents document original federal land transfers to private ownership

source_profiles.source_name: Fond du Lac County Probate Court Records
source_profiles.repository: Fond du Lac County Probate Court, Fond du Lac
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Wills, administrations, inventories, guardianships, and estate settlements for Fond du Lac County from 1836; Probate Court has jurisdiction over estate matters; WHS has microfilm of historical probate records

source_profiles.source_name: Fond du Lac County Marriage Records -- County Clerk / WHS / FamilySearch
source_profiles.repository: Fond du Lac County Clerk, Fond du Lac / Wisconsin Historical Society, Madison
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage records for Fond du Lac County from 1836; County Clerk issued marriage licenses in Wisconsin; WHS holds some county marriage records on microfilm; FamilySearch has indexed Wisconsin marriages; state vital records registration began 1907 (sporadic earlier compliance)

source_profiles.source_name: Fond du Lac County Federal and State Census Records
source_profiles.repository: NARA / FamilySearch / Ancestry / WHS
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal census 1840-1950 for Fond du Lac County; Wisconsin state census 1855, 1865, 1875, 1885, 1895, 1905 at WHS (unusually detailed -- include place of birth, occupation, citizenship status); territorial census 1836 and 1842 may include Fond du Lac County entries

source_profiles.source_name: Fond du Lac County German Church Records -- Catholic / Lutheran Parishes
source_profiles.repository: Local parishes / Catholic Archdiocese of Milwaukee or Diocese archives / Wisconsin Historical Society, Madison
source_profiles.coverage_notes: German Catholic and Lutheran parish registers (baptisms, marriages, burials) for Fond du Lac County; many parish registers kept in German until the 1900s; Catholic records through archdiocese/diocese archives; Lutheran records (Missouri Synod, Wisconsin Synod) through church archives; WHS has transcribed some German church records; essential for German immigrant family research

source_profiles.source_name: Fond du Lac County Cemetery Records -- Find A Grave / WI GenWeb
source_profiles.repository: Find A Grave / Wisconsin GenWeb / Fond du Lac County Historical Society
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Cemetery transcriptions for Fond du Lac County including church cemeteries, municipal cemeteries, and pioneer family burial grounds; Find A Grave has growing coverage; Wisconsin GenWeb and local historical societies have published transcriptions; DAR chapters have documented many Wisconsin cemeteries
